registry.description: Character is the pattern of the inner person made consistent through time and testing — not a single act or moment but the accumulated shape of who a person actually is when pressure is applied. The Greek term in this registry (dokimē) carries the sense of tested and proven quality, the character that has been refined rather than merely claimed. Character is what remains when the performance stops. It is the inner consistency between what a person professes and what they do when no one is watching.
registry.inference_note: Character as a psychological construct — the sum of stable moral and psychological traits — is ABSENT as a single biblical term. Scripture addresses character through the formed inner person, the fruit of the Spirit, and terms for moral quality.

terms.lsj_entry:
δοκῐμάζω, 
(δόκιμος) assay, test, πορφύραν καὶ χρυσόν [Refs 5th c.BC+]:—middle, prove for oneself, choose, χώραν [Refs 5th c.BC+]:— passive, ἐπειδὰν τὸ ἔργον.. δοκιμασθῇ [Refs]
__2 of persons, δ. αὐτούς put them to the test, make trial of them, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; also of Apis-bulls, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__II approve, sanction, μετὰ δεδοκιμασμένου [λόγου] μὴ ξυνέπεσθαι [Refs 5th c.BC+]infinitive, ἐκπονεῖν ἐδοκίμαζε he approved of their working, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__II.2 as a political term, 
__II.2.a approve after scrutiny as fit for an office, [Refs 5th c.BC+]:—passive, to be approved as fit, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; μου δοκιμαζομένου when I was undergoing a scrutiny, [Refs 4th c.BC+]; δεδοκιμασμένος [ἰατρός] [Refs 2nd c.AD+]: metaphorically, ὃν ὁ Ἥφαιστος ἐδοκίμασεν [Refs 2nd c.BC+]; ὑπὲρ τοῦ στεφανωθῆναι δοκιμάζομαι [Refs 4th c.BC+]
__II.2.b pass as fit to serve, ἱππεύειν δεδοκιμασμένος [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__II.2.c examine and admit boys to the class of ἔφηβοι or ἔφηβοι to the rights of manhood, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__II.2.d test an orator's right to speak [Refs]
__II.3 with infinitive, think fit to do, [Refs 1st c.AD+]: with negative, refuse to do, [NT+1st c.AD+]

senses.sense_text:
testing, trying, examination 
proof, probation, testing,trying, examination, Heb. 3:9*

verses.verse_text: Luk 12:56 You hypocrites ! You know how to interpret the appearance of earth and sky , but why do you not know how to interpret the present time ?
Luk 14:19 And another said , ‘I have bought five yoke of oxen , and I go to examine them . Please have me excused .’
Rom 1:28 And since they did not see fit to acknowledge God , God gave them up to a debased mind to do what ought not to be done.
Rom 2:18 and know his will and approve what is excellent , because you are instructed from the law ;
Rom 12:2 Do not be conformed to this world , but be transformed by the renewal of your mind , that by testing you may discern what is the will of God , what is good and acceptable and perfect .
Rom 14:22 The faith that you have , keep between yourself and God . Blessed is the one who has no reason to pass judgment on himself for what he approves .
1Cor 3:13 each one’s work will become manifest , for the Day will disclose it, because it will be revealed by fire , and the fire will test what sort of work each one has done.
1Cor 11:28 Let a person examine himself , then , and so eat of the bread and drink of the cup .
1Cor 16:3 And when I arrive , I will send those whom you accredit by letter to carry your gift to Jerusalem .
2Cor 8:8 I say this not as a command , but to prove by the earnestness of others that your love also is genuine .
2Cor 8:22 And with them we are sending our brother whom we have often tested and found earnest in many matters , but who is now more earnest than ever because of his great confidence in you .

2Cor 13:5 Examine yourselves , to see whether you are in the faith . Test yourselves . Or do you not realize this about yourselves , that Jesus Christ is in you ?— unless indeed you fail to meet the test !
Gal 6:4 But let each one test his own work , and then his reason to boast will be in himself alone and not in his neighbor .
Eph 5:10 and try to discern what is pleasing to the Lord .
Phili 1:10 so that you may approve what is excellent , and so be pure and blameless for the day of Christ ,
1Th 2:4 but just as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the gospel , so we speak , not to please man , but to please God who tests our hearts .
1Th 5:21 but test everything ; hold fast what is good .
1Ti 3:10 And let them also be tested first ; then let them serve as deacons if they prove themselves blameless .
Heb 3:9 where your fathers put me to the test and saw my works for forty years .

1Pe 1:7 so that the tested genuineness of your faith —more precious than gold that perishes though it is tested by fire —may be found to result in praise and glory and honor at the revelation of Jesus Christ .
1Jo 4:1 Beloved , do not believe every spirit , but test the spirits to see whether they are from God , for many false prophets have gone out into the world .

terms.lsj_entry:
δοκῐμ-ή, ἡ, 
proof, test, interpolated in [Refs 1st c.AD+]
__2 tried or approved character, [NT]

senses.sense_text:
character, test, proof 
trial, proof by trial, 2Cor. 8:2; the state or disposition of that which has been tried and approved, approved character or temper, Rom. 5:4; 2Cor. 2:9; Phil. 2:22; proof, document, evidence, 2Cor. 8:2; 13:3*

verses.verse_text: Rom 5:4 and endurance produces character , and character produces hope ,
2Cor 2:9 For this is why I wrote , that I might test you and know whether you are obedient in everything .
2Cor 8:2 for in a severe test of affliction , their abundance of joy and their extreme poverty have overflowed in a wealth of generosity on their part.

2Cor 9:13 By their approval of this service , they will glorify God because of your submission that comes from your confession of the gospel of Christ , and the generosity of your contribution for them and for all others,
2Cor 13:3 since you seek proof that Christ is speaking in me . He is not weak in dealing with you, but is powerful among you .
Phili 2:22 But you know Timothy’s proven worth , how as a son with a father he has served with me in the gospel .

terms.lsj_entry:
δοκῐμ-εῖον, τό, 
test, means of testing, [Refs 5th c.BC+] (variant δοκίμιον, as in [NT+1st c.BC+]; proof, εὐσεβείας [Refs 1st c.BC+]

senses.sense_text:
testing, proved genuineness 
that by means of which anything is tried, proof, criterion, test; trial, the act of trying, or putting to proof, Jas. 1:3; approved character, 1Pet. 1:7*

terms.lsj_entry:
δόκῐμ-ος, ον 
(Doric dialect α, ον [Refs], (δέχομαι) acceptable: hence, 
__1 of persons, trustworthy, [Refs 1st c.AD+]; approved, esteemed, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; δοκιμώτατος Ἑλλάδι most approved by Hellas, her noblest son, [Refs 5th c.BC+]: with infinitive, of approved ability to do.., δόκιμος δ᾽ οὔτις.. εἴργειν [Refs 4th c.BC+]
__2 of things, excellent, τὸ ἔαρ -ώτατον [Refs 5th c.BC+]; notable, considerable, ποταμός [Refs]; approved, κριθὰ καθαρὰ δ. [Refs]; δ. ἀργύριον legal tender, [Refs 4th c.BC+]; ὕμνος acceptable, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__3 adverb -μως really, genuinely, [Refs 5th c.BC+]

senses.sense_text:
approved by testing, genuine 
proved, tried; approved, after examination and trial, Rom. 16:10; Jas. 1:12; by implication acceptable, Rom. 14:18

verses.verse_text: Rom 14:18 Whoever thus serves Christ is acceptable to God and approved by men .
Rom 16:10 Greet Apelles , who is approved in Christ . Greet those who belong to the family of Aristobulus .
1Cor 11:19 for there must be factions among you in order that those who are genuine among you may be recognized .
2Cor 10:18 For it is not the one who commends himself who is approved , but the one whom the Lord commends .
2Cor 13:7 But we pray to God that you may not do wrong — not that we may appear to have met the test , but that you may do what is right , though we may seem to have failed .
2Ti 2:15 Do your best to present yourself to God as one approved , a worker who has no need to be ashamed , rightly handling the word of truth .
Jam 1:12 Blessed is the man who remains steadfast under trial , for when he has stood the test he will receive the crown of life , which God has promised to those who love him .
